content:
{
  "title": "Dynamic Test-Driven Prompting for Enhanced Code Generation with Large Language Models",
  "problem": "Current code generation prompts for LLMs often fail to capture nuanced requirements and edge case handling, leading to suboptimal functional correctness. There is a need for prompting methods that better guide models to produce robust, high-quality code.",
  "motivation": "As LLM-powered coding tools see increasing adoption, the cost of generated code errors rises, especially in security and correctness-critical domains. Most existing prompting methods rely on natural language descriptions or static examples, which may inadequately specify requirements. A prompting technique that dynamically scaffolds code generation using tailored test cases could better align outputs with user intent.",
  "method": "We propose a 'dynamic test-driven prompting' method where, prior to code generation, the user provides (or the model generates) a suite of input-output test cases that define the expected behavior. The prompt interleaves these tests with natural language requirements and examples, and instructs the model to write code that passes all tests. Optionally, an automated verifier (external or LLM-based) can augment the prompt with hints for failing cases as feedback loops. This method can be implemented by templated prompt structures and, optionally, iterative prompting with updated test results.",
  "experiment_plan": "We evaluate on HumanEval, MBPP, and APPS datasets. Baselines include standard natural language only, example-based, and chain-of-thought prompting. Metrics are pass@k (k=1,5,10), functional correctness, and test suite coverage. Ablation studies assess the impact of test-driven prompting and feedback loops."
}
